limits: A positive decision only means a separate governed integration plan may be proposed.
Does not modify context selection or ContextPackManifest.items.
Does not change provider prompts or prompt rendering.
Does not persist graph nodes or graph edges.
Does not introduce a graph database.
Does not call retrieval services, embedding services, rerankers, or production indexes.
Does not mutate indexes, memory stores, runtime manifests, or repository graph state.
Does not perform automatic context inclusion.
Does not admit or implement context-pack integration.
name: Opt-in graph-neighborhood context-pack candidates
recommended_next_actions: Use CAP-0009 to decide whether to propose a separate context-pack integration plan.
Do not treat CAP-0009 output as runtime selection authority.
risks: A positive context-pack candidate integration may be mistaken for integration approval unless the separate-plan boundary remains explicit.
Future context-pack integration, graph persistence, retrieval, embeddings, reranking, prompt changes, or runtime selection require separate governed plans and warrants.
summary: Provides an opt-in, default-off graph-neighborhood context candidate path inside build_latest_context_manifest. The slice converts read-only graph-neighborhood advisor candidates into ordinary context-pack items only when explicitly enabled, while preserving existing default context-pack behavior and avoiding provider prompt, runtime manifest, retrieval, embedding, reranking, persisted graph, index, memory-store, and automatic context inclusion changes.
